name: ssot-tb-cocotb
description: SSOT->TB cocotb: derive a well-structured pyuvm/cocotb verification environment from YAML and RTL, run simulation, emit VCD/coverage, or escalate precise failures

tasks.content: [SSOT->pyuvm/cocotb] Parse SSOT, RTL, and build verification architecture ledger
tasks.detail: Read <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml, <ip>/verify/equivalence_goals.json when present, <ip>/list/<ip>.f, and every referenced RTL source before writing tests. If equivalence_goals.json is missing, run or request /ssot-equiv-goals <ip> before claiming TB signoff. Build a compact ledger from SSOT top_module, parameters, io_list, registers, features, dataflow, function_model, cycle_model, timing, security, error_handling, debug_observability, integration, dft, synthesis, memory, interrupts, fsm, test_requirements.scenarios, quality_gates, and equivalence goals. For each scenario/goal, record stimulus, expected result, checker, coverage bin, required protocol agent, sequence item fields, driver responsibility, monitor observation point, scoreboard prediction source from function_model, cycle/waveform expectation from cycle_model, timing timeout/performance expectation, security/error negative checks, debug waveform probes, quality-gate evidence owner, and any SSOT ambiguity. Do not assume DMA/APB/CPU/register-only shapes unless the SSOT says so.
tasks.criteria:
SSOT file was read
equivalence_goals.json was read or a precise /ssot-equiv-goals blocker was emitted
RTL filelist and all referenced RTL files were read
function_model transactions/invariants are mapped into scoreboard/reference-model checks
Every equivalence goal has a scoreboard, coverage, or explicit blocked/human-gate mapping
cycle_model latency/handshake/pipeline/ordering rules are mapped into cycle checks, timeouts, or waveform checks
timing/security/error/debug/integration constraints are mapped to tests, waveform probes, or precise evidence-gap escalations
quality_gates are split into TB-owned evidence and downstream EDA/signoff evidence
Every DUT top port is mapped to a cocotb signal, pyuvm agent connection, or intentional tie-off
Every SSOT scenario has stimulus, expected result, checker, coverage bin, sequence/driver/monitor/scoreboard mapping
No fixed IP-specific generator/template was added

tasks.detail: Write a structured environment under <ip>/tb/cocotb/. Because pyuvm is available in the normal ATLAS environment, prefer real pyuvm components: sequence item/transaction, one or more sequences, sequencer, driver, monitor, scoreboard, coverage collector, env, and test class. If pyuvm import is unavailable in the runtime, fall back to cocotb-native Python classes with the same UVM-style layering and emit the fallback reason in the result. Required files: test_<ip>.py, test_runner.py, uvm_env.py or split equivalents such as transactions.py, sequences.py, agents.py, scoreboard.py, coverage.py, plus run_sim.py if needed for stable simulator/PATH setup. Use bounded write passes to avoid giant one-shot output: first transactions/coverage/scoreboard, then agents/sequences/env, then a compact test_<ip>.py that uses a SCENARIOS table and reusable helpers. Do not stop after support files: test_<ip>.py and test_runner.py are mandatory. Include clock/reset helpers, protocol agents derived from io_list, CSR/memory helpers when SSOT defines them, scoreboard/reference model derived from function_model transactions and invariants through workflow/tb-gen/runtime/equivalence_scoreboard.py (`EquivalenceScoreboard`) or a copied helper, reusable protocol monitors from workflow/tb-gen/runtime/protocol_monitors.py when ip_contract requires serial_frame_monitor, uart_frame_monitor, or chip_select_monitor, cycle/timeout checks derived from cycle_model, one pyuvm sequence/test or subtest per SSOT scenario, equivalence-goal checking, structured scoreboard event logging to <ip>/sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl, functional coverage bin collection, results.xml generation, and VCD setup for sim_debug. The runner must support bounded single-scenario selection before full regression, either through a test filter, scenario argument, or a generated one-scenario wrapper module; it must never require an unbounded full regression to prove the harness. Do not write one flat monolithic cocotb test unless the SSOT has exactly one trivial scenario and no protocol/state. Any helper that logs [FAIL], records failed checks, or sees expected/got mismatch must raise AssertionError.
tasks.criteria:
test_<ip>.py and test_runner.py exist and are non-empty
A pyuvm-based environment exists with sequence item, sequence, driver, monitor, scoreboard, coverage collector, env, and test class, or a documented cocotb-native UVM-style fallback exists if pyuvm cannot import
python3 -m py_compile <ip>/tb/cocotb/*.py passes
python3 workflow/tb-gen/runtime/equivalence_scoreboard.py <ip> --root . --self-check passes
Clock/reset behavior matches SSOT
DUT toplevel and parameter/port mapping match RTL
function_model drives scoreboard/reference expected values through EquivalenceScoreboard or an equivalent model adapter
equivalence_goals.json drives scoreboard goal IDs and pass criteria
scoreboard writes <ip>/sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl rows with goal_id, scenario_id, cycle, stimulus, fl_expected, rtl_observed, passed, mismatch, and coverage_refs
cycle_model drives latency, handshake, pipeline, ordering, timeout, or waveform checks
Every SSOT scenario is represented by executable pyuvm sequence/test logic
The runner can execute one named SSOT scenario with a wall-clock timeout before full regression
Every checker has explicit expected/got assertion text
Coverage JSON and VCD output paths are implemented
No RTL files were modified

tasks.content: [SSOT->pyuvm/cocotb] Run simulation and repair TB-only issues
tasks.activeForm: Running cocotb simulation from SSOT - attempt {loop_count}/{max_loop_iterations}
tasks.detail: Run the documented cocotb command after generation, but never start with an unbounded full regression. First run one reset/default scenario with a wall-clock timeout <= 30s, then one representative transfer/protocol scenario with a wall-clock timeout <= 60s, then the full SSOT regression with a documented timeout. Preferred command shape: python3 <ip>/tb/cocotb/run_sim.py --scenario <SCxx> --timeout-sec <N>. If no run_sim.py is needed, use a pytest/cocotb-test runner that supports an equivalent scenario filter and subprocess timeout. Avoid shell pipelines such as `vvp | tail`, `grep | head`, or `make | tail` for proof runs because they hide hangs and can prevent result XML from being written. The final report must state the exact bounded command used. If compile/elaboration fails due to TB syntax, runner paths, include paths, port mapping, pyuvm component wiring, or scoreboard bugs, fix only TB/runner files and rerun the bounded scenario. If a scenario fails, times out, or is interrupted against SSOT expected behavior, do not weaken the checker or change expected values; emit [SIM ESCALATE] -> rtl-gen with exact test, expected, got/timeout, last visible signal/FSM evidence, and suspected RTL signal/file.
tasks.criteria:
A bounded reset/default scenario command was run after the last TB edit
A bounded transfer/protocol scenario command was run or a precise escalation explains why it could not run
Full regression is run only after bounded smoke scenarios terminate
Compile/elaboration exits 0 for the bounded scenario, or the failure is escalated with evidence
All SSOT scenario tests pass OR failures/timeouts are escalated precisely
A PASS summary is rejected if any [FAIL], timeout, interrupted simulator, failed-check counter, or failed scenario remains
results.xml or sim_report.txt is fresh
The exact bounded command is documented for replay
TB does not modify RTL files

tasks.detail: On PASS path, ensure <ip>/sim/<ip>.vcd, <ip>/sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl, <ip>/cov/coverage_functional.json, <ip>/sim/coverage_report.md, and latest results XML exist. TB-gen owns raw functional-bin evidence only. The generic coverage stage owns final <ip>/cov/coverage.json by merging coverage_functional.json with <ip>/cov/fcov_plan.json and any tool coverage. Do not add IP-specific coverage harnesses or one-off parsers; static line/branch/FSM coverage belongs to the generic coverage workflow or must be reported as an evidence gap.
tasks.criteria:
VCD exists and is non-empty
scoreboard_events.jsonl exists and references equivalence goal IDs
coverage_functional.json exists with raw functional bin status
coverage_report.md exists and labels coverage source accurately
results.xml or sim_report.txt exists
TB-gen did not claim final SSOT coverage signoff by overwriting coverage.json
No IP-specific coverage workaround script was added

tasks.detail: PASS branch: emit [SSOT RESULT] tb-gen DONE with module, SSOT path, pyuvm/cocotb files, exact simulation command, pass count, coverage path, VCD path, and next workflow sim_debug. FAIL branch: emit one [SIM ESCALATE] -> rtl-gen block per failing behavior with module, file/signal if known, test, expected, got, and hypothesis. Do not claim DONE if any failure or evidence gap remains.
tasks.criteria:
DONE path includes fresh simulation evidence
DONE path names exact pyuvm/cocotb architecture files, VCD, and coverage files
Failure path includes precise SIM ESCALATE blocks
Next workflow is sim_debug on PASS
